description: A Chinese stone rubbing or ink painting depicting a seated Buddhist figure (the 16th Arhat, Cudapanthaka) in dark robes beside a massive gnarled tree trunk. The figure is shown in profile with exaggerated features — a large nose and pronounced brow — gesturing with one hand. A small lion-like creature sits at the base of the tree. Chinese calligraphic inscriptions fill the upper portions of the composition. The style is characteristic of the Five Dynasties period painter Guanxiu, known for his distinctive portrayals of arhats with eccentric, non-idealized features.
